title: Working Tree, Index, Commits, and Atomic History · Command Recall
summary: Turn a mixed set of feature, formatting, and accidental changes into reviewable commits without losing work. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an atomic commit construction workbook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace HEAD, index, and working-tree versions of a path before using status, diff, add, restore, or commit.
Read branch, staged, unstaged, untracked, rename, conflict, and ahead/behind status without treating short output as context-free.
Select working-tree, staged, commit-to-commit, path-limited, word, and summary diffs from the review question.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff.

Inspect the Atomic Commit Message trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for create a cohesive commit with an imperative summary, rationale, test evidence, and no accidental generated or secret data.

content.text: For Atomic Commit Message, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Atomic Commit Message precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A large commit is acceptable when its message lists every changed file.

stimulus.title: Atomic Commit Message case record

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create a cohesive commit with an imperative summary, rationale, test evidence, and no accidental generated or secret data. Artifact under review: an atomic commit construction workbook.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Atomic Commit Message, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Three Tree Model evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Three Tree Model assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Three Tree Model has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Three Tree Model precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Atomic Commit Message: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create a cohesive commit with an imperative summary, rationale, test evidence, and no accidental generated or secret data.

hints: Name the Atomic Commit Message precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
